heading: Seditious conspiracy
label: 2384 Seditious conspiracy
text: If two or more persons in any State or Territory, or in any place subject to the jurisdiction of the United States, conspire to overthrow, put down, or to destroy by force the Government of the United States, or to levy war against them, or to oppose by force the authority thereof, or by force to prevent, hinder, or delay the execution of any law of the United States, or by force to seize, take, or possess any property of the United States contrary to the authority thereof, they shall each be fined under this title or imprisoned not more than twenty years, or both.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 18, U.S.C., 1940 ed., § 6 (, ).Mar. 4, 1909, ch. 321, § 635 Stat. 1089
Amendments1994— substituted “fined under this title” for “fined not more than $20,000”.Pub. L. 103–3221956—Act , substituted “$20,000” for “$5,000”, and “twenty years” for “six years”.July 24, 1956

Effective Date of 1956 Amendment, , provided that:Act July 24, 1956, ch. 678, § 370 Stat. 624“The foregoing amendments [amending this section and ] shall apply only with respect to offenses committed on and after the date of the enactment of this Act [].”section 2385 of this titleJuly 24, 1956
